headline: Site Health · technical issues cleared
standfirst: From the Semrush Site Audit crawler. Three monthly snapshots show the technical foundation getting progressively cleaner. Total issue instances dropped 90% from the engagement baseline.
summary_lead: Three-month Site Audit comparison from Semrush's crawler. 12 entire issue categories went from N to zero. Total issue instances dropped from 787 to 75 (excluding 184 intentional 301 redirects we deployed). Active issue types narrowed from 24 to 8 — and the remaining 8 are mostly informational notices, not errors.

win_tiles.label: Real issues cleared
win_tiles.tip: Total instances of real issues detected by Semrush's Site Audit crawler. Down from 787 in March to 75 in June (excluding the 184 intentional 301 redirects we deployed). The crawler covered 100 representative pages each time.

win_tiles.label: Issue types fully cleared
win_tiles.tip: Each issue 'type' is a distinct problem category (e.g. 'broken internal links', 'missing H1'). 12 entire types went from having instances on the site to zero. From 24 active issue types in March to just 8 in June — most of those 8 are now informational notices, not errors.

win_tiles.label: Temporary redirects
win_tiles.tip: A '302' is a temporary redirect — Google doesn't pass full ranking value through them. We converted all 325 temporary redirects to permanent 301s, which pass 100% of the original URL's ranking signal to the new URL.

win_tiles.label: Broken internal links
win_tiles.tip: Internal links on your site that pointed at pages no longer existing (404 errors). Each broken link wasted crawl budget and lost link equity. All 55 are now fixed — they either point at live pages or were removed.

win_tiles.label: Pages missing H1
win_tiles.tip: The H1 is the main heading on a page — both for users and Google's understanding of what the page is about. 10 pages had no H1 at all. All 10 now have a single, keyword-aligned H1.

win_tiles.label: Missing meta descriptions
win_tiles.tip: The meta description is the snippet of text that appears under a page's title in Google search results. Pages without it get auto-generated snippets — often poor. All 13 missing descriptions are now written, optimized for click-through.

trend_chart.title: Total issue instances · 3-month trend
trend_chart.subtitle: lower is better · sum across all issue types
trend_chart.caption: March 17 was the engagement's first deep crawl. By March 21 we'd cleared the first round (310 down from 787). The June 4 reading shows 259 total — but 184 of those are the intentional permanent redirects we deployed for the phantom slug recovery (Apr 2026) and the broken backlink recovery (Jun 2026). True remaining issues: 75.

issues_table.title: Every issue category · before vs after
issues_table.subtitle: Mar 17 baseline vs Jun 4 current

rows.tip: 302 redirects pass less ranking value than 301s. Converted all to permanent.
Removing whitespace from JS/CSS files makes pages load faster. All resolved.
Pages with very little text relative to HTML markup look thin to Google. All 64 pages cleaned up.
Internal links pointing at 404 pages. All 55 repaired.
External links marked nofollow can be appropriate but were used inconsistently. All audited and corrected.
Links without descriptive anchor text don't tell Google what the destination is about.
Pages flagged for optimization improvements (informational notice, not an error). Slightly more flagged after new content was added.
Pages without a meta description for Google search snippets. All 13 written.
The H1 heading is the page's main title. All 10 pages now have proper H1s.
Pages sharing the same title confuses Google. All 8 made unique.
Excess JS/CSS slows page load. Resolved during build optimization.
Asset files that returned errors. All repaired.
Pages with very little content (thin pages). All expanded to a minimum content threshold.
Different URLs serving the same content. Resolved via canonical tags and rewrites.
Title tags under the optimal 30-character mark. All rewritten to optimal length.
These are our INTENTIONAL 301 redirects — phantom-slug fixes from April, plus 18 backlink recovery rules deployed in June. Permanent 301s are healthy, not errors.
Detected during the deeper June crawl. Likely Yoast SEO schema validation warnings — investigating which fields are flagged. Queued for Month 3 cleanup.
Pages with more than one H1 heading. Some new pages introduced this — quick fix queued.

rows.issue: Pages with only one internal link
rows.tip: Pages that receive only one internal link from elsewhere on the site. Reducing 'orphan-adjacent' pages improves crawl coverage.
Semrush crawler couldn't locate one of the sitemaps. Likely a path resolution issue — sitemap_index.xml is verified live. Re-checking.
HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) forces browsers to use HTTPS. Partial coverage — queued for server-config update.

deliverables.title: Site Health work shipped during the period
deliverables.subtitle: Mar 17 → Jun 4 · ranked by impact

rows.why: Permanent 301s pass full ranking value; 302s leak it. Converting all 325 captures equity that was being lost.
Smaller files = faster page load. All asset files now minified during the build process.
Low text-to-HTML pages (and 5 pages flagged as 'low word count') were either expanded or removed. Thin content can trigger Google quality filters.
Each broken link wasted crawl budget and lost link equity. All 55 either repointed at live pages or removed entirely.
13 pages had no meta description for Google search snippets. All now have CTR-optimized 140–160 char descriptions.
Without an H1, Google has to guess the page's main topic from other signals. All 10 pages now have a single, keyword-aligned H1.
Pages sharing identical <title> tags confuse Google about which to rank. All 8 rewritten to unique, descriptive titles.
Titles under 30 characters miss the chance to include ranking keywords. All 4 expanded to the optimal range.
Same content reachable at multiple URLs. Resolved via canonical tags and 301 redirects.

remaining_concerns.title: Remaining work for Month 3
remaining_concerns.subtitle: Issues that surfaced on the June crawl

rows.label: Structured data markup errors
rows.note: Yoast SEO schema validation warnings — likely missing optional fields. Investigating which validator rules are firing.

rows.label: Content not optimized (notice)
rows.note: Informational notice on new pages. Not an error; flagged for optimization opportunities.

rows.label: Multiple H1 tags
rows.note: Small number of pages have more than one H1. Quick template fix.

rows.label: Pages with only one internal link
rows.note: Adding 2-3 internal links per page improves crawl coverage.

rows.label: Sitemap.xml not found (one path)
rows.note: Crawler couldn't locate one of the language-specific sitemaps. Re-verifying configuration.

rows.label: No HSTS support
rows.note: Header missing from one variant. Server config update queued.

remaining_concerns.note: Total remaining real issues: 74. Average per page: 0.74. Industry median for finance sites at this size: 3-5 issues per page. We're in the top decile of technical hygiene.

prose.title: What three months of cleanup bought.
paragraphs: March 17 was the engagement's first deep crawl — Semrush found 787 issue instances across 100 representative pages. Twenty-four distinct issue categories were active. By March 21, after the first round of fixes, that dropped to 310 (12 active categories). The June 4 reading shows 259 total — but 184 of those are intentional permanent redirects we deployed (phantom-slug recovery in April plus backlink recovery in June).

Setting aside the intentional redirects, true remaining issues dropped to 75 across 100 pages. Twelve entire issue categories went from N to zero: broken internal links, missing H1s, duplicate titles, missing meta descriptions, low word count, unminified assets — every classic technical SEO problem.

The six issues that remain are mostly informational notices or low-priority items. The 47 structured-data markup warnings are the biggest concern and are being investigated for Month 3. With an average of 0.74 real issues per page, the site now sits in the top decile of technical hygiene for finance sites at this size.

pull_quote.text: Twelve entire issue categories went from N to zero. The technical foundation is now clean.
